_comment: Token-savings benchmark fixture for /gaia-create-story script-tier wiring (E63-S11 / Work Item 6 / AC6). Tokens are estimated from word counts (English avg ~1.33 tokens/word) of the SKILL.md prose at each commit, plus a fixed-overhead estimate for subagent dispatch (PM Derek + Architect Theo + optional UX Designer Christy) and Val (opus-pinned per ADR-074 contract C2). The two SKILL.md commits are byte-pinned for audit traceability — the prose-line/word delta IS the token-savings claim, since the per-invocation subagent overhead and Val pass are unchanged across the two revisions. Re-capture trigger: any SKILL.md change > 50 LOC.
baseline_commit_summary: feat(E61-S2): wire gaia-create-story Step 4 to read sizing_map for points derivation — last commit before E63 prose-growth landed
post_commit_summary: feat(E63-S11): SKILL.md thin-orchestrator rewrite — prose-to-script invocations across ten deterministic operations
methodology: tokens_estimate = words * 1.33 (English avg); SKILL.md prose word counts taken via `wc -w` at the two pinned commits; subagent dispatch overhead (PM + Architect ± UX, plus Val opus pass) held constant at 1200 words across both runs since neither E61-S2 nor E63-S11 changed the dispatch payloads. The savings claim equals the prose delta — that is the load-bearing portion of the V1↔V2 token-cost gap closure.
